questions.question: What is a 'Vanity Metric' in content marketing?

options.text: A metric that directly results in revenue
A high-volume metric like 'Likes' or 'Views' that feels good but doesn't necessarily indicate business growth
A metric tracked in a private spreadsheet
The number of hours spent writing

questions.explanation: Vanity metrics can be misleading. 10,000 views from the wrong audience is less valuable than 10 views from your ideal customer.

questions.question: What does the 'Share of Model' metric measure in the AEO era?

options.text: How many employees you have
How often AI models (ChatGPT, Perplexity) cite your content as the answer to niche questions
The percentage of your traffic coming from Google
The amount of content written by AI

questions.explanation: As more users turn to AI for answers, being the 'cited source' (Share of Model) becomes as important as being #1 in traditional search.

questions.question: Which of these is the most valuable metric for a solo founder to track?

options.text: Total unique page views
Average session duration
Conversion rate from Visitor to Lead (Email Signup)
The number of images in a post

questions.explanation: At the end of the day, your content engine exists to build your lead pipeline. Capturing the relationship is the most important conversion point.

questions.question: What is the simplest way to perform 'Manual Attribution' for a new sale?

options.text: Guessing based on the date
Asking the customer 'How did you hear about us?' during their first call or in a form
Checking Google Analytics only
Assuming everyone found you on Twitter

questions.explanation: No technical tool is 100% accurate. Asking the customer directly provides the highest-quality 'First-Touch' attribution data.

questions.question: Why is 'Scroll Depth' a useful engagement metric?

options.text: It shows how fast the user can read
It indicates if users are actually reaching your CTAs at the middle or bottom of your content
It helps with SEO ranking
It counts as a page view

questions.explanation: If people leave after the first 20% of the post, they aren't seeing your conversion points. Scroll depth helps you identify where you're losing their attention.

questions.question: Define your 'North Star' content metric. Is it newsletter signups, trial starts, or demo bookings? Why did you pick this specific metric? How will tracking this change the way you write your next 'Source Piece'?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate if the metric is 'Valuable' vs 'Vanity'. It must be a lead-capture or revenue-generating activity. Push back if they choose 'Followers' or 'Views'.
